Sherley Project — Carboplatin Cohort

Author

Tianqi Ouyang

Published

April 9, 2026

1 Setup

Code 
# Force UTF-8 locale so kable captions and other strings render real Unicode
# (e.g. em-dash) instead of <U+2014> escape sequences.
invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_ALL", "en_US.UTF-8"))
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidycmprsk)
library(ggsurvfit)
library(gtsummary)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(rvg)

# Output directory for downloadable Word tables (placed under docs/ so the
# rendered website can serve them from ../tables/carbo/<name>.docx).
docx_dir <- "docs/tables/carbo"
dir.create(docx_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Output directory for downloadable PowerPoint plots (../plots/carbo/<name>.pptx).
# Use absolute path so fig.process hook (which may run from a different
# working directory than the chunks themselves) still resolves correctly.
pptx_dir <- normalizePath("docs/plots/carbo", winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(pptx_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

save_tbl_docx <- function(tbl, docx_name) {
  ft <- gtsummary::as_flex_table(tbl)
  flextable::save_as_docx(ft, path = file.path(docx_dir,
                                               paste0(docx_name, ".docx")))
  invisible(tbl)
}

# Save the given ggplot as an editable PowerPoint slide via rvg::dml.
# The chunk label (from knitr::opts_current) is used as the filename so each
# chunk produces a uniquely-named pptx in docs/plots/carbo/. Returns NULL on
# failure (officer/rvg missing, ggplot invalid, etc.). Plot helpers below
# call this directly; for inline ggplot chunks the fig.process hook below
# also runs it on ggplot2::last_plot() as a safety net.
.save_plot_pptx <- function(p, name = NULL) {
  if (is.null(p) || !inherits(p, "ggplot")) return(invisible(NULL))
  if (is.null(name)) {
    name <- tryCatch(knitr::opts_current$get("label"), error = function(e) NULL)
  }
  if (is.null(name)) return(invisible(NULL))
  safe <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", name)
  out  <- file.path(pptx_dir, paste0(safe, ".pptx"))
  ok <- tryCatch({
    pp <- officer::read_pptx()
    pp <- officer::add_slide(pp, layout = "Title and Content", master = "Office Theme")
    pp <- officer::ph_with(
      pp,
      value = rvg::dml(ggobj = p),
      location = officer::ph_location_type(type = "body")
    )
    print(pp, target = out)
    TRUE
  }, error = function(e) FALSE)
  invisible(if (ok) paste0("../plots/carbo/", safe, ".pptx") else NULL)
}

# Safety-net fig.process hook: after each chunk's figure is saved as PNG,
# also save ggplot2::last_plot() as .pptx. Plot helpers also call
# .save_plot_pptx directly inside their bodies; this hook covers inline-
# ggplot chunks (eGFR histogram, dose-distribution, etc.) that don't go
# through a helper. Safe to double-save — same chunk label, same target file.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.process = function(file_path) {
  lbl <- tryCatch(knitr::opts_current$get("label"), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(lbl)) {
    p <- tryCatch(ggplot2::last_plot(), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(p) && inherits(p, "ggplot")) {
      .save_plot_pptx(p, lbl)
    }
  }
  file_path
})

# Per-plot download links are injected after render by the post-render
# script scripts/inject_pptx_links.py (configured in _quarto.yml). It
# scans each rendered HTML page, finds every figure-html <img> whose
# basename matches a .pptx slug in docs/plots/carbo/, and inserts a
# small "📥 Download editable PowerPoint" link beneath the figure.
# (We tried doing this inside R via knitr hooks, but Quarto's HTML
# pipeline bypasses both the plot and chunk hooks, so a post-render
# pass on the HTML is the reliable path.)

# Fallback renderers for when a model fit fails (singular design matrix etc.)
# on the small Sherley sub-cohort. Used by tryCatch wrappers in fg_model,
# csh_model, cox_model, and the plot_* helpers. Saves a placeholder pptx so
# the download index still lists the figure (so reviewers know which model
# could not be fit).
.plot_failed <- function(label = "Model could not be fit") {
  p <- ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
             label = paste0(label, "\n(singular design — cohort too small)"),
             size = 3.5) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_void()
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}
.tbl_failed <- function() {
  knitr::asis_output(
    "> *Model could not be fit — design matrix is singular for this sub-cohort.*"
  )
}

# Load whole-cohort data, then keep only carboplatin patients
dat <- readRDS("sherley_final_master.rds") %>%
  filter(platin_group == "Carboplatin")

# Create eGFR ratio: CKD-EPI Cr-Cys (unindexed) / Cockcroft-Gault
# Also create per-10 mL/min rescaled versions of cockcroft and CKD-EPI for
# reporting (HRs per 1 mL/min are clinically uninterpretable; convention in
# nephro-onc is per 10 mL/min — bio_agent confirmed). Adjusters keep raw scale.
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    # Capped versions (for dose discrepancy calculations)
    cockcroft_cap125 = pmin(cockcroft, 125),
    # ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex_cap125 already exists in data
    # Ratio uses UNCAPPED eGFR
    egfr_ratio    = ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex / cockcroft,
    egfr_ratio_per10 = -egfr_ratio * 10,  # per 0.1-unit *decrease* reporting scale
    egfr_discrepancy = ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex - cockcroft,
    # Dose discrepancy uses CAPPED eGFR (already computed in sherley_data.qmd)
    dose_discrep_per25increase = dose_discrepancy / 25,
    abs_dose_discrep = abs(dose_discrepancy),
    abs_dose_discrep_cat = cut(abs_dose_discrep,
      breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100, Inf),
      labels = c("<25", "25-49.99", "50-74.99", "75-99.99", ">=100"),
      right = FALSE, include.lowest = TRUE),
    # Binary exposure for adjusted CIF curves (Section 14): 1 if patient was
    # over-dosed by 25 mg or more (actual - predicted >= 25), else 0.
    dose_discrep_ge25 = as.integer(dose_discrepancy >= 25),
    # Signed dose discrepancy = actual - predicted; 10 bins symmetric around 0.
    signed_dose_discrep_cat = cut(dose_discrepancy,
      breaks = c(-Inf, -100, -75, -50, -25, 0, 25, 50, 75, 100, Inf),
      labels = c("<=-100", "-99.99 to -75", "-74.99 to -50",
                 "-49.99 to -25", "-24.99 to 0",
                 "0 to 24.99", "25 to 49.99", "50 to 74.99",
                 "75 to 99.99", ">=100"),
      right = FALSE, include.lowest = TRUE),
    cockcroft_per10 = cockcroft / 10,
    ckd_epi_per10   = ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex / 10
  )

cat("Carboplatin cohort N =", nrow(dat), "\n")
Carboplatin cohort N = 121

2 Dose Distribution (Carboplatin)

2.1 Actual vs Predicted Dose

Code 
dose_long <- dat %>%
  select(record_id, carbo_dose_redcap, carbo_dose_cre_cys) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(carbo_dose_redcap, carbo_dose_cre_cys),
    names_to = "dose_type", values_to = "dose_mg"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(dose_mg)) %>%
  mutate(dose_type = case_match(dose_type,
    "carbo_dose_redcap"  ~ "Actual (REDCap)",
    "carbo_dose_cre_cys" ~ "Predicted (CKD-EPI Cr-Cys)"
  ))

ggplot(dose_long, aes(x = dose_mg, fill = dose_type)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.5, bins = 40, position = "identity", color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Actual (REDCap)" = "#E31A1C",
                                "Predicted (CKD-EPI Cr-Cys)" = "#1F78B4")) +
  labs(title = "Carboplatin Dose Distribution",
       x = "Dose (mg)", y = "Number of Patients", fill = "Dose Type") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

2.2 Signed Dose Discrepancy Distribution

Smooth kernel density estimate of the signed dose discrepancy (actual − predicted, CKD-EPI Cr-Cys). The area under the curve is shaded by sign — negative (under-dosed region) on one side, positive on the other.

Code 
dose_vals <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(dose_discrepancy)) %>%
  pull(dose_discrepancy)

dens <- density(dose_vals, n = 512)
y_at_zero <- approx(dens$x, dens$y, xout = 0)$y

dens_df <- tibble(x = dens$x, y = dens$y) %>%
  bind_rows(tibble(x = 0, y = y_at_zero)) %>%
  arrange(x)

neg_part <- dens_df %>% filter(x <= 0) %>% mutate(side = "neg")
pos_part <- dens_df %>% filter(x >= 0) %>% mutate(side = "pos")
plot_df  <- bind_rows(neg_part, pos_part)

ggplot() +
  geom_area(data = plot_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = side), alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_line(data = dens_df, aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.6, color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(neg = "#E31A1C", pos = "#1F78B4"),
                    guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-250, 250)) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Signed Dose Discrepancy (Carboplatin)",
       x = "Dose Discrepancy (mg) = actual - predicted",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

3 Table 1: Baseline Characteristics (Carboplatin Only)

Code 
dat %>%
  dplyr::select(
    age, age_cat, sex, race, ethnicity, tumor_stage, cancer_type_cat_7,
    carbo_AUC, carbo_AUC_cat,
    ici, Bevacizumab, Paclitaxel, Pemetrexed, Doxorubicin, Etoposide,
    Gemcitabine, Pertuzumab, Trastuzumab, Docetaxel, other_chemo, Administered_alone,
    hiv_rpdr, thyroid, smoking, steroids,
    cci, score, ecog_score, bmi, bmi_cat,
    pre_HGB_45days, pre_PLT_45days, pre_ALB_45days,
    baseline_cys_c, baseline_cre, cr_cys,
    baseline_cre_egfr, baseline_cys_c_egfr,
    ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys, ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex,
    cockcroft, egfr_ratio
  ) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    statistic = list(age ~ "{mean} ({sd})")
  )

4 eGFR Ratio Distribution (Carboplatin)

Code 
ratio_hist <- dat %>% filter(!is.na(egfr_ratio))

ggplot(ratio_hist, aes(x = egfr_ratio)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(count) / sum(after_stat(count)) * 100),
                 binwidth = 0.1, boundary = 0,
                 fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of eGFR Ratio (Carboplatin)",
       x = "eGFR Ratio",
       y = "Percent of Patients (%)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 3, 0.2)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

Event Counts by Outcome (Diagnostic)

For each outcome the table below shows the cohort N, events (status = 1 within 90 days), competing deaths (status = 2; not applicable to 90-day death which is modelled by Cox PH), censored, event rate, and events-per-variable (EPV) using the 5-variable base adjuster set for this cohort (age, sex, ecog_score (ECOG performance status, numeric 0–5), ici, cockcroft (creatinine clearance)). Following the Jiaxuan-paper methodology, anemia models additionally adjust for pre_HGB_45days (baseline hemoglobin within 45 days), thrombocytopenia models additionally adjust for pre_PLT_45days (baseline platelets within 45 days), and the composite Grade 2 / Grade 3 cytopenia models (which combine anemia, thrombocytopenia, and AKI events) adjust for both pre_HGB_45days and pre_PLT_45days — so anemia / throm outcomes carry 6 adjusters and the composites carry 7. Read the EPV column against the per-outcome adjuster count, not the base 5. As a rule of thumb, Fine-Gray fits need ≥ 5 × adj events to be stable; < 2 × adj almost always produces a singular design matrix, which is why the helper functions in this file fall back to a “Model could not be fit” placeholder where you see one.

Code 
.outcomes_tbl <- function(dat, cohort_label, n_adj = 5) {
  sumrow <- function(label, status_var, is_cox = FALSE) {
    s <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(as.character(dat[[status_var]])))
    n_total     <- sum(!is.na(s))
    n_event     <- sum(s == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_compdeath <- if (is_cox) NA_integer_ else sum(s == 2, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_cens      <- sum(s == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    epv         <- if (n_total > 0) n_event / n_adj else NA_real_
    fit_likely  <- if (is.na(epv))                "—"
                   else if (n_event >= 5 * n_adj) "✓ likely OK"
                   else if (n_event >= 2 * n_adj) "marginal"
                   else                           "✗ singular likely"
    tibble::tibble(
      Outcome            = label,
      N                  = n_total,
      Events             = n_event,
      `Competing deaths` = n_compdeath,
      Censored           = n_cens,
      `Event rate`       = if (n_total > 0) sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * n_event / n_total) else "—",
      EPV                = if (!is.na(epv)) sprintf("%.1f", epv) else "—",
      `Fit reliable?`    = fit_likely
    )
  }
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    sumrow("Anemia Grade 2",                   "cmp_status_anemia_2"),
    sumrow("Anemia Grade 3",                   "cmp_status_anemia_3"),
    sumrow("Thrombocytopenia Grade 2",         "cmp_status_throm_2"),
    sumrow("Thrombocytopenia Grade 3",         "cmp_status_throm_3"),
    sumrow("AKI Grade 2",                      "cmp_status_aki_2"),
    sumrow("AKI (KDIGO)",                      "cmp_status_aki_kdigo"),
    sumrow("Composite Grade 2 AEs",            "cmp_status_grade_2_aes"),
    sumrow("Composite Grade 3 AEs",            "cmp_status_grade_3_aes"),
    sumrow("Overall Hospitalization",          "cmp_status_any_hosp"),
    sumrow("Platinum-Related Hospitalization", "cmp_status_side_effect"),
    sumrow("90-Day Death (Cox PH)",            "death_90", is_cox = TRUE)
  )
}

knitr::kable(
  .outcomes_tbl(dat, "carboplatin cohort"),
  align = c("l","r","r","r","r","r","r","l"),
  caption = sprintf(
    "Event counts for the **carboplatin cohort** (N = %d). EPV uses 5 base adjusters (age, sex, ecog_score, ici, cockcroft); anemia / thrombocytopenia models add pre_HGB_45days / pre_PLT_45days for 6 total.",
    nrow(dat)
  )
)
Event counts for the carboplatin cohort (N = 121). EPV uses 5 base adjusters (age, sex, ecog_score, ici, cockcroft); anemia / thrombocytopenia models add pre_HGB_45days / pre_PLT_45days for 6 total.
Outcome N Events Competing deaths Censored Event rate EPV Fit reliable?
Anemia Grade 2 121 42 5 74 34.7% 8.4 ✓ likely OK
Anemia Grade 3 121 14 8 99 11.6% 2.8 marginal
Thrombocytopenia Grade 2 121 16 7 98 13.2% 3.2 marginal
Thrombocytopenia Grade 3 121 10 8 103 8.3% 2.0 marginal
AKI Grade 2 121 8 9 104 6.6% 1.6 ✗ singular likely
AKI (KDIGO) 121 11 9 101 9.1% 2.2 marginal
Composite Grade 2 AEs 121 47 5 69 38.8% 9.4 ✓ likely OK
Composite Grade 3 AEs 121 16 8 97 13.2% 3.2 marginal
Overall Hospitalization 121 29 4 88 24.0% 5.8 ✓ likely OK
Platinum-Related Hospitalization 121 21 5 95 17.4% 4.2 marginal
90-Day Death (Cox PH) 121 10 NA 111 8.3% 2.0 marginal

5 Fine-Gray Models — eGFR Ratio (Carboplatin)

Each table below shows univariable (one variable at a time) and multivariable Fine-Gray subdistribution hazard ratios side-by-side. A Word version of every table is saved during render — click any link below to download:

  • Anemia Grade 2 · Anemia Grade 3
  • Thrombocytopenia Grade 2 · Thrombocytopenia Grade 3
  • AKI Grade 2 · AKI (KDIGO)
  • Composite Grade 2 AEs · Composite Grade 3 AEs
  • Overall Hospitalization · Platinum-Related Hospitalization
  • 90-Day Death (Cox PH)
Code 
.adj_set <- function(pre_labs, egfr_adj) {
  # Sherley sub-cohort (N=169) adjuster set:
  #   - 5 base confounders: age, sex, ecog_score, ici, cockcroft
  #   - + pre_labs when supplied — anemia models pass "pre_HGB_45days"
  #     and thrombocytopenia models pass "pre_PLT_45days" to match the
  #     Jiaxuan-paper methodology (baseline hemoglobin / platelets).
  # The egfr_adj argument is accepted for backward compatibility but
  # cockcroft is already in the base set so it is effectively ignored.
  adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  if (!is.null(pre_labs)) adj <- c(adj, pre_labs)
  adj
}

# fg_model: returns univariable + multivariable merged Fine-Gray table.
# If docx_name is supplied, also saves the table as a .docx in docx_dir.
fg_model <- function(time_var, status_var, pred_var, pre_labs = NULL,
                     egfr_adj = c("none", "cockcroft", "ckd_epi"),
                     docx_name = NULL) {
  egfr_adj <- match.arg(egfr_adj)
  adj <- .adj_set(pre_labs, egfr_adj)
  all_vars <- c(pred_var, adj)

  fmla_mv <- as.formula(paste0(
    "Surv(", time_var, ", ", status_var, ") ~ ",
    paste(all_vars, collapse = " + ")
  ))
  tbl_mv <- tryCatch(
    tidycmprsk::crr(fmla_mv, failcode = 1, data = dat) %>%
      tbl_regression(exp = TRUE,
                     pvalue_fun = function(x) style_pvalue(x, digits = 3)) %>%
      add_n(),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  y_call <- str2lang(sprintf("Surv(%s, %s)", time_var, status_var))
  tbl_uv <- tryCatch(
    rlang::inject(
      tbl_uvregression(
        data = dat,
        method = tidycmprsk::crr,
        y = !!y_call,
        include = dplyr::all_of(all_vars),
        exponentiate = TRUE,
        pvalue_fun = function(x) style_pvalue(x, digits = 3),
        method.args = list(failcode = 1)
      )
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(tbl_uv) && is.null(tbl_mv)) {
    return(knitr::asis_output(
      "> *Model could not be fit — Fine-Gray design matrix is singular (insufficient events or collinear covariates for this sub-cohort).*"
    ))
  }

  out <- if (is.null(tbl_mv)) {
    tbl_uv
  } else if (is.null(tbl_uv)) {
    tbl_mv
  } else {
    tbl_merge(
      tbls = list(tbl_uv, tbl_mv),
      tab_spanner = c("**Univariable**", "**Multivariable**")
    )
  }

  if (!is.null(docx_name)) save_tbl_docx(out, docx_name)
  out
}

cox_model <- function(time_var, status_var, pred_var, pre_labs = NULL,
                      egfr_adj = c("none", "cockcroft", "ckd_epi"),
                      docx_name = NULL) {
  egfr_adj <- match.arg(egfr_adj)
  adj <- .adj_set(pre_labs, egfr_adj)
  all_vars <- c(pred_var, adj)

  fmla_mv <- as.formula(paste0(
    "Surv(", time_var, ", ", status_var, ") ~ ",
    paste(all_vars, collapse = " + ")
  ))
  tbl_mv <- survival::coxph(fmla_mv, data = dat) %>%
    tbl_regression(exp = TRUE,
                   pvalue_fun = function(x) style_pvalue(x, digits = 3)) %>%
    add_n()

  y_call <- str2lang(sprintf("Surv(%s, %s)", time_var, status_var))
  tbl_uv <- rlang::inject(
    tbl_uvregression(
      data = dat,
      method = survival::coxph,
      y = !!y_call,
      include = dplyr::all_of(all_vars),
      exponentiate = TRUE,
      pvalue_fun = function(x) style_pvalue(x, digits = 3)
    )
  )

  out <- tbl_merge(
    tbls = list(tbl_uv, tbl_mv),
    tab_spanner = c("**Univariable**", "**Multivariable**")
  )

  if (!is.null(docx_name)) save_tbl_docx(out, docx_name)
  out
}

6 Cause-Specific Hazard Models — eGFR Ratio (Carboplatin)

Code 
csh_model <- function(time_var, status_var, pred_var, pre_labs = NULL) {
  adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  if (!is.null(pre_labs)) adj <- c(adj, pre_labs)
  d <- dat
  d$csh_status <- ifelse(d[[status_var]] == 1, 1L, 0L)
  fmla <- as.formula(paste0(
    "Surv(", time_var, ", csh_status) ~ ",
    paste(c(pred_var, adj), collapse = " + ")
  ))
  tryCatch(
    survival::coxph(fmla, data = d) %>%
      tbl_regression(exp = TRUE,
                     pvalue_fun = function(x) style_pvalue(x, digits = 3)) %>%
      add_n(),
    error = function(e) .tbl_failed()
  )
}

cockcroft 121 1.01 0.99, 1.03 0.357 121 0.99 0.97, 1.01 0.294

ici 121 0.73 0.21, 2.60 0.632 121 1.16 0.27, 5.08 0.842
cockcroft 121 1.01 0.99, 1.03 0.357 121 0.99 0.97, 1.01 0.562
1 HR = Hazard Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval
NoteWhy anchor at the median (subHR = 1 at the reference)

Three reasons to anchor at the median:

  1. Interpretability: “A patient with eGFR ratio of 0.8 has ~1.5× the subHR compared to a typical patient (at the median).”
  2. Stability: The median sits in the densest part of the data, so estimates near it have the tightest confidence intervals. The CIs fan out as you move away — you can see this in the plots.
  3. No arbitrary choice: If we picked 1.0 arbitrarily, the plot would look almost identical here (because median ≈ 1), but in cohorts where the median is 0.9 or 1.1, picking 1.0 would either shift or distort the curve without changing the actual model.

9 Linear subHR Curves — eGFR Ratio (raw scale) (Carboplatin)

Code 
plot_subhr_linear_ratio <- function(time_var, status_var, extra_adj = NULL,
                                    outcome_label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  keep <- c("egfr_ratio", time_var, status_var, base_adj, extra_adj)
  d <- dat %>% dplyr::select(all_of(keep)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()

  adj_df <- d[, c(base_adj, extra_adj), drop = FALSE]
  X_adj <- model.matrix(~ ., data = adj_df)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  X <- cbind(egfr_ratio = d$egfr_ratio, X_adj)

  stat_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(as.character(d[[status_var]])))
  time_num <- as.numeric(d[[time_var]])

  fit <- tryCatch(
    cmprsk::crr(ftime = time_num, fstatus = stat_num,
                cov1 = X, failcode = 1),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(outcome_label))

  beta   <- fit$coef[1]
  V_beta <- fit$var[1, 1]

  ref_x  <- median(d$egfr_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  grid_x <- seq(quantile(d$egfr_ratio, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
                quantile(d$egfr_ratio, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 100)

  log_hr <- beta * (grid_x - ref_x)
  se     <- sqrt((grid_x - ref_x)^2 * V_beta)

  df_plot <- tibble(
    x     = grid_x,
    subhr = exp(log_hr),
    lcl   = exp(log_hr - 1.96 * se),
    ucl   = exp(log_hr + 1.96 * se)
  )

  ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = subhr)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +
    geom_line(color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = ref_x, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    labs(x = "CKD-EPI Cr-Cys / CG ratio",
         y = "subHR (95% CI)",
         title = outcome_label) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.65, 1.45), ylim = c(0, 4)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

plot_cox_linear_ratio <- function(time_var, status_var, extra_adj = NULL,
                                  outcome_label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  keep <- c("egfr_ratio", time_var, status_var, base_adj, extra_adj)
  d <- dat %>% dplyr::select(all_of(keep)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()

  fmla <- as.formula(paste0(
    "Surv(", time_var, ", ", status_var, ") ~ egfr_ratio + ",
    paste(c(base_adj, extra_adj), collapse = " + ")
  ))
  fit <- tryCatch(survival::coxph(fmla, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(outcome_label))

  beta   <- coef(fit)["egfr_ratio"]
  V_beta <- vcov(fit)["egfr_ratio", "egfr_ratio"]

  ref_x  <- median(d$egfr_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  grid_x <- seq(quantile(d$egfr_ratio, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
                quantile(d$egfr_ratio, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 100)

  log_hr <- beta * (grid_x - ref_x)
  se     <- sqrt((grid_x - ref_x)^2 * V_beta)

  df_plot <- tibble(
    x   = grid_x,
    hr  = exp(log_hr),
    lcl = exp(log_hr - 1.96 * se),
    ucl = exp(log_hr + 1.96 * se)
  )

  ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = hr)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +
    geom_line(color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = ref_x, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    labs(x = "CKD-EPI Cr-Cys / CG ratio",
         y = "HR (95% CI)",
         title = outcome_label) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.65, 1.45), ylim = c(0, 4)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

9.1 Anemia Grade 2

Code 
plot_subhr_linear_ratio("cmp_time_anemia_2", "cmp_status_anemia_2",
                        "pre_HGB_45days", "Anemia Grade 2 (Carboplatin)")

                      NULL, "90-Day Death (Cox PH) (Carboplatin)")

10 Linear subHR Curves — eGFR Discrepancy (raw scale) (Carboplatin)

Code 
plot_subhr_linear_egfrdisc <- function(time_var, status_var, extra_adj = NULL,
                                       outcome_label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  keep <- c("egfr_discrepancy", time_var, status_var, base_adj, extra_adj)
  d <- dat %>% dplyr::select(all_of(keep)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()

  adj_df <- d[, c(base_adj, extra_adj), drop = FALSE]
  X_adj <- model.matrix(~ ., data = adj_df)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  X <- cbind(egfr_discrepancy = d$egfr_discrepancy, X_adj)

  stat_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(as.character(d[[status_var]])))
  time_num <- as.numeric(d[[time_var]])

  fit <- tryCatch(
    cmprsk::crr(ftime = time_num, fstatus = stat_num,
                cov1 = X, failcode = 1),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(outcome_label))

  beta   <- fit$coef[1]
  V_beta <- fit$var[1, 1]
  se_b   <- sqrt(V_beta)

  q1    <- as.numeric(quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE))
  q3    <- as.numeric(quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE))
  iqr_w <- q3 - q1
  hr1   <- exp(beta);             hr1_l <- exp(beta - 1.96 * se_b);             hr1_u <- exp(beta + 1.96 * se_b)
  hri   <- exp(beta * iqr_w);     hri_l <- exp(beta * iqr_w - 1.96 * abs(iqr_w) * se_b); hri_u <- exp(beta * iqr_w + 1.96 * abs(iqr_w) * se_b)
  subtitle_str <- sprintf(
    "subHR per 1 mL/min: %.2f (%.2f-%.2f)  |  per IQR (Δ = %.1f): %.2f (%.2f-%.2f)  |  IQR: [%.1f, %.1f]",
    hr1, hr1_l, hr1_u, iqr_w, hri, hri_l, hri_u, q1, q3)

  ref_x  <- median(d$egfr_discrepancy, na.rm = TRUE)
  grid_x <- seq(quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
                quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 100)

  log_hr <- beta * (grid_x - ref_x)
  se     <- sqrt((grid_x - ref_x)^2 * V_beta)

  df_plot <- tibble(
    x     = grid_x,
    subhr = exp(log_hr),
    lcl   = exp(log_hr - 1.96 * se),
    ucl   = exp(log_hr + 1.96 * se)
  )

  ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = subhr)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +
    geom_line(color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = ref_x, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(q1, q3), linetype = "dashed",
               color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(x = "eGFR Discrepancy (mL/min)",
         y = "subHR (95% CI)",
         title = outcome_label,
         subtitle = subtitle_str) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 4)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey25")) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

plot_cox_linear_egfrdisc <- function(time_var, status_var, extra_adj = NULL,
                                     outcome_label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  keep <- c("egfr_discrepancy", time_var, status_var, base_adj, extra_adj)
  d <- dat %>% dplyr::select(all_of(keep)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()

  fmla <- as.formula(paste0(
    "Surv(", time_var, ", ", status_var, ") ~ egfr_discrepancy + ",
    paste(c(base_adj, extra_adj), collapse = " + ")
  ))
  fit <- tryCatch(survival::coxph(fmla, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(outcome_label))

  beta   <- coef(fit)["egfr_discrepancy"]
  V_beta <- vcov(fit)["egfr_discrepancy", "egfr_discrepancy"]
  se_b   <- sqrt(V_beta)

  q1    <- as.numeric(quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE))
  q3    <- as.numeric(quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE))
  iqr_w <- q3 - q1
  hr1   <- exp(beta);             hr1_l <- exp(beta - 1.96 * se_b);             hr1_u <- exp(beta + 1.96 * se_b)
  hri   <- exp(beta * iqr_w);     hri_l <- exp(beta * iqr_w - 1.96 * abs(iqr_w) * se_b); hri_u <- exp(beta * iqr_w + 1.96 * abs(iqr_w) * se_b)
  subtitle_str <- sprintf(
    "HR per 1 mL/min: %.2f (%.2f-%.2f)  |  per IQR (Δ = %.1f): %.2f (%.2f-%.2f)  |  IQR: [%.1f, %.1f]",
    hr1, hr1_l, hr1_u, iqr_w, hri, hri_l, hri_u, q1, q3)

  ref_x  <- median(d$egfr_discrepancy, na.rm = TRUE)
  grid_x <- seq(quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
                quantile(d$egfr_discrepancy, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 100)

  log_hr <- beta * (grid_x - ref_x)
  se     <- sqrt((grid_x - ref_x)^2 * V_beta)

  df_plot <- tibble(
    x   = grid_x,
    hr  = exp(log_hr),
    lcl = exp(log_hr - 1.96 * se),
    ucl = exp(log_hr + 1.96 * se)
  )

  ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = hr)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +
    geom_line(color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = ref_x, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(q1, q3), linetype = "dashed",
               color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(x = "eGFR Discrepancy (mL/min)",
         y = "HR (95% CI)",
         title = outcome_label,
         subtitle = subtitle_str) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 4)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey25")) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

                           "pre_HGB_45days", "Anemia Grade 2 (Carboplatin)")

11 Linear subHR Curves — Dose Discrepancy (raw scale) (Carboplatin)

Code 
plot_subhr_linear <- function(time_var, status_var, extra_adj = NULL,
                              outcome_label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  keep <- c("dose_discrepancy", time_var, status_var, base_adj, extra_adj)
  d <- dat %>% dplyr::select(all_of(keep)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()

  adj_df <- d[, c(base_adj, extra_adj), drop = FALSE]
  X_adj <- model.matrix(~ ., data = adj_df)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  X <- cbind(dose_discrepancy = d$dose_discrepancy, X_adj)

  stat_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(as.character(d[[status_var]])))
  time_num <- as.numeric(d[[time_var]])

  fit <- tryCatch(
    cmprsk::crr(ftime = time_num, fstatus = stat_num,
                cov1 = X, failcode = 1),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(outcome_label))

  beta   <- fit$coef[1]
  V_beta <- fit$var[1, 1]

  ref_x  <- median(d$dose_discrepancy, na.rm = TRUE)
  grid_x <- seq(quantile(d$dose_discrepancy, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
                quantile(d$dose_discrepancy, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 100)

  log_hr <- beta * (grid_x - ref_x)
  se     <- sqrt((grid_x - ref_x)^2 * V_beta)

  df_plot <- tibble(
    x     = grid_x,
    subhr = exp(log_hr),
    lcl   = exp(log_hr - 1.96 * se),
    ucl   = exp(log_hr + 1.96 * se)
  )

  ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = subhr)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +
    geom_line(color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = ref_x, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    labs(x = "Dose Discrepancy (mg)",
         y = "subHR (95% CI)",
         title = outcome_label) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 4)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

plot_cox_linear <- function(time_var, status_var, extra_adj = NULL,
                            outcome_label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  keep <- c("dose_discrepancy", time_var, status_var, base_adj, extra_adj)
  d <- dat %>% dplyr::select(all_of(keep)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()

  fmla <- as.formula(paste0(
    "Surv(", time_var, ", ", status_var, ") ~ dose_discrepancy + ",
    paste(c(base_adj, extra_adj), collapse = " + ")
  ))
  fit <- tryCatch(survival::coxph(fmla, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(outcome_label))

  beta   <- coef(fit)["dose_discrepancy"]
  V_beta <- vcov(fit)["dose_discrepancy", "dose_discrepancy"]

  ref_x  <- median(d$dose_discrepancy, na.rm = TRUE)
  grid_x <- seq(quantile(d$dose_discrepancy, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
                quantile(d$dose_discrepancy, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 100)

  log_hr <- beta * (grid_x - ref_x)
  se     <- sqrt((grid_x - ref_x)^2 * V_beta)

  df_plot <- tibble(
    x   = grid_x,
    hr  = exp(log_hr),
    lcl = exp(log_hr - 1.96 * se),
    ucl = exp(log_hr + 1.96 * se)
  )

  ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = hr)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +
    geom_line(color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = ref_x, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    labs(x = "Dose Discrepancy (mg)",
         y = "HR (95% CI)",
         title = outcome_label) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 4)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

                        NULL, "Electrolyte Abnormalities (Carboplatin)")

                        NULL, "Cancer Progression (Carboplatin)")

13.9 Immune-related AE & Other

Composite competing-risk outcome combining hospitalisations coded as Immune-related AE (reason 9) and Other (reason 11). Event = 1 if either occurred within 90 days; time = earliest of the two.

Code 
plot_subhr_linear_ratio("cmp_time_hosp_immune_other",
                        "cmp_status_hosp_immune_other",
                        NULL, "Immune-related AE & Other (Carboplatin)")

13.10 Planned Surgery/Port

Code 
plot_subhr_linear_ratio("cmp_time_hosp_10", "cmp_status_hosp_10",
                        NULL, "Planned Surgery/Port (Carboplatin)")

13 Scatter Plots — Dose Discrepancy vs eGFR (Carboplatin)

13.1 Dose Discrepancy vs CKD-EPI Cr-Cys (unindexed)

Code 
ggplot(dat %>% filter(!is.na(dose_discrepancy)),
       aes(x = ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex, y = dose_discrepancy)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "CKD-EPI Cr-Cys eGFR (unindexed, mL/min)",
       y = "Dose Discrepancy (mg)",
       title = "Dose Discrepancy vs CKD-EPI Cr-Cys eGFR (Carboplatin)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

13.2 Dose Discrepancy vs Cockcroft-Gault

Code 
ggplot(dat %>% filter(!is.na(dose_discrepancy)),
       aes(x = cockcroft, y = dose_discrepancy)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "Cockcroft-Gault eGFR (mL/min)",
       y = "Dose Discrepancy (mg)",
       title = "Dose Discrepancy vs Cockcroft-Gault eGFR (Carboplatin)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

14 Adjusted CIF — Dose Discrepancy >= +25 mg (Carboplatin)

Standardized cumulative incidence functions for each competing-risk outcome, contrasting patients with dose_discrepancy >= 25 (red) against those with dose_discrepancy < 25 (blue). The Fine-Gray model is fit with the binary exposure plus the same adjuster set used in Section 7. Curves are adjusted (standardized) via g-computation: for every patient, predict CIF under exposure = 1 and under exposure = 0, then average the two CIF curves over the cohort. Y-axis is cumulative incidence, x-axis is days from baseline.

90-day death (Section 14.11) is modelled with Cox PH instead of Fine-Gray (no competing event), and the standardized cumulative-incidence-of-death curve is 1 − S(t|Xᵢ) averaged across the cohort.

Code 
adj_cif <- function(time_var, status_var, extra_pre = NULL, label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  vars <- c("dose_discrep_ge25", base_adj, extra_pre)
  d <- dat %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(c(time_var, status_var, vars))) %>%
    tidyr::drop_na()

  fmla <- as.formula(paste("~",
            paste(c("dose_discrep_ge25", base_adj, extra_pre), collapse = " + ")))
  X_full <- model.matrix(fmla, data = d)[, -1, drop = FALSE]

  stat_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(as.character(d[[status_var]])))
  time_num <- as.numeric(d[[time_var]])

  fit <- tryCatch(
    cmprsk::crr(ftime = time_num, fstatus = stat_num,
                cov1 = X_full, failcode = 1),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(label))

  # subHR (95% CI) for the binary exposure, extracted from the same FG fit
  # dose_discrep_ge25 is the first column of cov1 (entered first in the formula)
  beta_e <- unname(fit$coef[1])
  se_e   <- sqrt(fit$var[1, 1])
  hr     <- exp(beta_e)
  lcl    <- exp(beta_e - 1.96 * se_e)
  ucl    <- exp(beta_e + 1.96 * se_e)
  pval   <- 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(beta_e / se_e)))
  p_str  <- ifelse(pval < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", pval))
  subtitle_str <- sprintf(
    "Discrep >= +25 vs <+25: subHR = %.2f (95%% CI %.2f-%.2f), p = %s",
    hr, lcl, ucl, p_str
  )

  X0 <- X_full; X0[, "dose_discrep_ge25"] <- 0
  X1 <- X_full; X1[, "dose_discrep_ge25"] <- 1

  pred0 <- predict(fit, X0)
  pred1 <- predict(fit, X1)

  times <- pred0[, 1]
  cif0  <- rowMeans(pred0[, -1, drop = FALSE])
  cif1  <- rowMeans(pred1[, -1, drop = FALSE])

  df <- bind_rows(
    tibble(time = times, cif = cif0, group = "Discrep < +25 mg"),
    tibble(time = times, cif = cif1, group = "Discrep >= +25 mg")
  )

  ymax <- max(df$cif, na.rm = TRUE)

  ggplot(df, aes(x = time, y = cif, color = group)) +
    geom_step(linewidth = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Discrep < +25 mg"  = "#1F78B4",
                                  "Discrep >= +25 mg" = "#E31A1C")) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(0.05, ymax * 1.05))) +
    labs(x = "Days from baseline",
         y = "Cumulative incidence",
         color = NULL,
         title = label,
         subtitle = subtitle_str) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "top",
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey25")) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

adj_death_curve <- function(time_var = "time_to_death_90",
                            status_var = "death_90", label) {
  base_adj <- c("age", "sex", "ecog_score", "ici", "cockcroft")
  vars <- c("dose_discrep_ge25", base_adj)
  d <- dat %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(c(time_var, status_var, vars))) %>%
    tidyr::drop_na()

  rhs <- paste(c("dose_discrep_ge25", base_adj), collapse = " + ")
  fmla <- as.formula(sprintf("survival::Surv(%s, %s) ~ %s",
                             time_var, status_var, rhs))
  fit <- tryCatch(survival::coxph(fmla, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(.plot_failed(label))

  beta_e <- unname(coef(fit)["dose_discrep_ge25"])
  se_e   <- sqrt(vcov(fit)["dose_discrep_ge25", "dose_discrep_ge25"])
  hr     <- exp(beta_e)
  lcl    <- exp(beta_e - 1.96 * se_e)
  ucl    <- exp(beta_e + 1.96 * se_e)
  pval   <- 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(beta_e / se_e)))
  p_str  <- ifelse(pval < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", pval))
  subtitle_str <- sprintf(
    "Discrep >= +25 vs <+25: HR = %.2f (95%% CI %.2f-%.2f), p = %s   (Cox PH)",
    hr, lcl, ucl, p_str
  )

  d0 <- d; d0$dose_discrep_ge25 <- 0
  d1 <- d; d1$dose_discrep_ge25 <- 1

  sf0 <- survival::survfit(fit, newdata = d0)
  sf1 <- survival::survfit(fit, newdata = d1)

  s0 <- if (is.matrix(sf0$surv)) sf0$surv else as.matrix(sf0$surv)
  s1 <- if (is.matrix(sf1$surv)) sf1$surv else as.matrix(sf1$surv)

  cif0 <- 1 - rowMeans(s0)
  cif1 <- 1 - rowMeans(s1)

  df <- bind_rows(
    tibble(time = sf0$time, cif = cif0, group = "Discrep < +25 mg"),
    tibble(time = sf1$time, cif = cif1, group = "Discrep >= +25 mg")
  )

  ymax <- max(df$cif, na.rm = TRUE)

  ggplot(df, aes(x = time, y = cif, color = group)) +
    geom_step(linewidth = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Discrep < +25 mg"  = "#1F78B4",
                                  "Discrep >= +25 mg" = "#E31A1C")) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(0.05, ymax * 1.05))) +
    labs(x = "Days from baseline",
         y = "Cumulative incidence (1 - S)",
         color = NULL,
         title = label,
         subtitle = subtitle_str) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "top",
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey25")) -> p
  .save_plot_pptx(p)
  p
}

14.1 Anemia Grade 2

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_anemia_2", "cmp_status_anemia_2",
        "pre_HGB_45days", "Anemia Grade 2")

14.2 Anemia Grade 3

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_anemia_3", "cmp_status_anemia_3",
        "pre_HGB_45days", "Anemia Grade 3")

14.3 Thrombocytopenia Grade 2

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_throm_2", "cmp_status_throm_2",
        "pre_PLT_45days", "Thrombocytopenia Grade 2")

14.4 Thrombocytopenia Grade 3

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_throm_3", "cmp_status_throm_3",
        "pre_PLT_45days", "Thrombocytopenia Grade 3")

14.5 AKI Grade 2

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_aki_2", "cmp_status_aki_2",
        NULL, "AKI Grade 2")

14.6 AKI (KDIGO)

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_aki_kdigo", "cmp_status_aki_kdigo",
        NULL, "AKI (KDIGO)")

14.7 Composite Grade 2 AEs

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_grade_2_aes", "cmp_status_grade_2_aes",
        c("pre_HGB_45days", "pre_PLT_45days"), "Composite Grade 2 AEs")

14.8 Composite Grade 3 AEs

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_grade_3_aes", "cmp_status_grade_3_aes",
        c("pre_HGB_45days", "pre_PLT_45days"), "Composite Grade 3 AEs")

14.9 Overall Hospitalization

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_any_hosp", "cmp_status_any_hosp",
        NULL, "Overall Hospitalization")

14.10 Platinum-Related Hospitalization

Code 
adj_cif("cmp_time_side_effect", "cmp_status_side_effect",
        NULL, "Platinum-Related Hospitalization")

14.11 90-Day Death (Cox PH)

Code 
adj_death_curve("time_to_death_90", "death_90", "90-Day Death")

15 Paper

A total of 44 (36.4%) patients received an excess dose of carboplatin, defined as ≥ 25 mg higher than the dose that would have been predicted using the CKD-EPI eGFRCR-CYS equation adjusted for actual BSA.

(Denominator: 121 carboplatin patients with a computable predicted dose.)

16 Diagnostic — Local/Early Stage Patients (Record IDs)

The carboplatin cohort contains a small number of patients with tumor_stage = Local/Early. Their record IDs are listed below — useful for QA / chart review.

Code 
local_early <- dat %>%
  dplyr::filter(as.character(tumor_stage) %in% c("Local/Early", "1")) %>%
  dplyr::select(record_id, EMPI, tumor_stage, cancer_type_cat_7, age, sex) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(record_id)

knitr::kable(
  local_early,
  align = c("r","r","l","l","r","l"),
  caption = sprintf("Carboplatin cohort patients with tumor_stage = Local/Early (N = %d).",
                    nrow(local_early))
)
Carboplatin cohort patients with tumor_stage = Local/Early (N = 6).
record_id EMPI tumor_stage cancer_type_cat_7 age sex
230 116619928 Local/Early Thoracic 67.73034 Female
299 100143576 Local/Early Thoracic 74.50119 Female
511 102298680 Local/Early Thoracic 78.44092 Female
582 100903629 Local/Early Thoracic 53.95046 Female
637 103516063 Local/Early Thoracic 60.24491 Male
835 100448808 Local/Early Thoracic 75.44029 Female

17 Downloadable PowerPoint Plots

Every figure on this page is also saved as an editable PowerPoint slide (vector graphics — text and shapes remain editable in PowerPoint). Click any link below to download the .pptx for that figure.

Code 
pptx_files <- list.files(pptx_dir, pattern = "\\.pptx$", full.names = FALSE)
if (length(pptx_files) == 0) {
  cat("*No PowerPoint files were produced during this render.*\n")
} else {
  for (f in sort(pptx_files)) {
    label <- gsub("_", " ", tools::file_path_sans_ext(f))
    cat(sprintf("- [📥 %s](../plots/carbo/%s)\n", label, f))
  }
}
